explicit_text: I moved to the United Arab Emirates when I was nineteen and lived there with my husband for twenty years. In 2009, the financial crisis hit the U.A.E., and my husband lost his job. Because of that, we had to leave the country and start a new chapter elsewhere.

explicit_text: After leaving the U.A.E., my children and I moved to New Delhi, India, with our eight children. New Delhi became a place where we faced cultural differences and new schools, shaping our understanding of change.

explicit_text: In July 2013, my children and I moved to the United States after our time in India ended. This move marked a new beginning for us, and it is why we are here today. Adjusting to life in the U.S. has taken time, but we are slowly settling in and finding stability.
